llm_result.summary: This plan targets six modifiable predictors derived from the case: external emotional demands, help-seeking difficulty, emotion suppression, delayed breakdowns after suppression, boundary fatigue/resentment, and social support. Each lever operates across BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains or their interactions to reduce burnout risk, improve help-seeking efficacy, enhance emotional processing, prevent crash episodes, and strengthen a sustainable support system. The solutions emphasize practical skills (boundaries, scripts, emotion expression), behavioral routines (sleep/energy management, reflection), cognitive-shift approaches (values-based renegotiation), and social infrastructure (support networks), with clear measurement, feasible feasibility estimates, and safety considerations.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured boundary-setting training with weekly decline scripts and boundary renegotiation plans; implement a personal boundary map and a 1-week boundary experiment (e.g., no unsolicited listening after 8pm).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces chronic external emotional demands and role fatigue by creating predictable limits, which can lower resentment (C05) and the frequency/intensity of others' demands (C01). Improves capacity to respond to distress without internal collapse.
solutions.measurement: Boundaries practiced per week (count); self-rated boundary effectiveness (0-10); days with boundary failures (0-7); perceived workload balance.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for relationship strain if boundaries are set abruptly; emphasize compassionate communication when declining requests.
solutions.mapping_query: Do regular boundary-setting practices reduce external demands (C01) and improve feelings of fatigue/resentment (C05) without compromising relationships?

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach brief, scripted requests for help and supportive language; practice with role-plays; implement a ‘help-asking trigger’ (e.g., when distressed, share a one-sentence need and one-minute guidance request).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves ability to seek support during distress, increasing perceived social support, reducing isolation, and decreasing the likelihood of suppression-driven strain and resentment.
solutions.measurement: Number of help-asks per week; self-efficacy in asking for help (0-10); perceived available support (0-10).
solutions.safety_notes: Requests should be within personal capacity; ensure alignment with boundaries to prevent overburdening others.
solutions.mapping_query: Does increasing help-seeking self-efficacy increase help-asking frequency and perceived support, attenuating C01 and C05?

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement daily emotion labeling and brief expressive-writing or venting with a trusted listener; 5-minute end-of-day check-in focusing on what felt difficult and why.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing habitual suppression improves emotional processing, decreases the buildup of negative affect, and lowers crash likelihood after suppression (C04).
solutions.measurement: Frequency of emotional disclosure (per week); suppression scale score (0-10); daily mood variability.
solutions.safety_notes: Encourage disclosure with safe, non-judgmental listener; escalate if distress increases.
solutions.mapping_query: Will increasing emotional expression reduce suppression (C03) and subsequent crashes (C04)?

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured energy budget with regular breaks, consistent light-dark cues, and sleep hygiene routines; daily energy and mood logging with a 2-week pre/post crash monitoring.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: By stabilizing arousal and restoration processes, the likelihood of delayed breakdowns after suppression decreases and daytime functioning improves.
solutions.measurement: Crashes per week; sleep quality rating (0-10); daily energy score (0-10).
solutions.safety_notes: Adjust sleep goals if nighttime sleep worsens; avoid aggressive caffeine use late in day.
solutions.mapping_query: Does energy/sleep stabilization reduce frequency/severity of crashes after suppression (C04)?

solutions.solution_candidate: Values clarification session followed by a renegotiation plan with key others; translate values into concrete boundary and delegation choices; implement a weekly reflection to monitor alignment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Aligning caregiving/strong-role expectations with personal values reduces resentment and fatigue from over-responsibility; smoother boundary renegotiation lowers external demands pressure.
solutions.measurement: Resentment score (0-10); proportion of self-initiated vs others-initiated support tasks; boundary adherence rating.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for relational conflict during renegotiation; ensure open, respectful communication.
solutions.mapping_query: Does values-aligned renegotiation reduce resentment (C05) and improve boundary functioning (C06) and perceived demands (C01)?

solutions.solution_candidate: Create a support-network map (3–5 reliable contacts), schedule regular check-ins, and pilot a brief social-prescribing approach (community groups, peer support).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Expanding the social buffer reduces sole reliance on self, distributes emotional labor, and buffers stress, reducing perceived demand and fatigue.
solutions.measurement: Number of active supportive connections; weekly check-in frequency; satisfaction with support (0-10).
solutions.safety_notes: Screen for toxic or draining relationships; promote healthy, reciprocal support.
solutions.mapping_query: Will expanding and maintaining a supportive network decrease external demand burden (C01) and related fatigue (C05)?
